questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow

Left turns are not allowed

Parking reserved for disabled permit holders

Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red

Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed

Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed

Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane

Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment

Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road

choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road

The road ahead is divided by a median

Two lanes are merging into one

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall

Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment

Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

A right curve with a side road joining ahead

A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down

Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane

A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead

An intersection is ahead

Railroad crossing — yield to trains

Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road

Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead

Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left

Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed

Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road

A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the light turns green
Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop only if other traffic is present
Yield to traffic on your left
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires you to come to a complete stop and stay stopped until the signal turns green.

questions.prompt: What should you do when you approach a steady yellow traffic light?
choices: Speed up to clear the intersection
Stop if you can do so safely before the intersection
Treat it as a flashing yellow and slow down
Come to a complete stop and wait for green
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that red is coming, so you should stop if you can do so safely rather than speed up.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing red traffic signal mean?
choices: The signal is broken, so proceed without stopping
Stop completely, then proceed when it is safe
Slow down but you do not need to stop
Yield only to pedestrians
questions.explanation: A flashing red light means the same as a stop sign: stop fully, then go when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: An eight-sided (octagonal) red sign always means what?
choices: Yield
Stop
Do not enter
Construction ahead
questions.explanation: An octagonal red sign is always a stop sign, requiring a complete stop.

questions.prompt: What does a triangular red and white sign mean?
choices: Stop
Yield the right-of-way
No passing
Wrong way
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is a yield sign, meaning you must slow and give the right-of-way to other traffic.

questions.prompt: What color are signs that warn of construction and work zones?
choices: Yellow
Orange
Blue
Green
questions.explanation: Orange signs mark construction and work zones so drivers slow down and watch for workers.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing yellow traffic signal mean?
choices: Stop completely before proceeding
Slow down and proceed with caution
The light is about to turn red
Make a complete stop like a stop sign
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light tells you to slow down and proceed carefully through the intersection.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the centerline means what?
choices: You may pass when the road ahead is clear
Do not pass
The lane is for turning only
Traffic flows in both directions in your lane
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side of the road means passing is prohibited there.

questions.prompt: What does a green arrow signal pointing left mean?
choices: You may turn left and oncoming traffic is stopped for you
You must yield to oncoming traffic before turning
Turning left is prohibited
Stop and wait for a solid green light
questions.explanation: A green left arrow gives you a protected turn, meaning oncoming traffic is being held by a red light.

questions.prompt: White lines on the roadway are used to separate what?
choices: Lanes of traffic moving in opposite directions
Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
The road from the sidewalk
Parking spaces only
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes of traffic traveling in the same direction, while yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, who has the right-of-way when two vehicles arrive at exactly the same time?
choices: The vehicle on the left
The vehicle on the right
The larger vehicle
The faster vehicle
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When you are turning left at an intersection, you must do what?
choices: Yield to oncoming traffic that is close enough to be a hazard
Proceed first because left turns have priority
Honk and turn quickly
Wait for all traffic in every direction to stop
questions.explanation: A driver turning left must yield to oncoming vehicles that are close enough to pose a danger.

questions.prompt: In Alabama, when may you make a right turn on a red light?
choices: Never
After a complete stop, if no sign prohibits it and the way is clear
Only between 6 a.m. and midnight
Without stopping if no cars are coming
questions.explanation: Alabama allows a right turn on red after a full stop, unless a sign prohibits it, once you yield to pedestrians and cross traffic.

questions.prompt: What must you do when approaching a pedestrian who is blind and using a white cane or guide dog?
choices: Honk to warn them
Always stop and yield
Proceed slowly without stopping
Drive around them quickly
questions.explanation: Drivers must always stop and yield to a pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog.

questions.prompt: When entering a road from a driveway or private road, you must do what?
choices: Yield to all traffic and pedestrians already on the road
Proceed first because you are entering
Stop only if a sign tells you to
Speed up to merge quickly
questions.explanation: Vehicles entering from a driveway or private road must yield to traffic and pedestrians already on the roadway.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at a crosswalk?
choices: The driver, who may proceed first
A pedestrian crossing in the crosswalk
Whoever arrives first
Larger vehicles only
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield to pedestrians in marked and unmarked crosswalks.

questions.prompt: When two roads meet and one is unpaved, which driver should yield?
choices: The driver on the paved road
The driver on the unpaved road
Neither driver
The driver going faster
questions.explanation: At an uncontrolled intersection of a paved and unpaved road, the driver on the unpaved road should yield.

questions.prompt: Before making any turn, what should you do?
choices: Signal your intention well before the intersection
Brake hard at the last moment
Speed up to complete the turn quickly
Move to the center of the road
questions.explanation: Signaling early warns other drivers and pedestrians of your intended turn.

questions.prompt: When you enter a roundabout, you should yield to whom?
choices: Traffic already circulating in the roundabout
Traffic waiting to enter behind you
No one, because you have priority
Only large trucks
questions.explanation: Vehicles entering a roundabout must yield to traffic already moving within it.

questions.prompt: At an intersection with a green light but heavy cross traffic blocking the way, what should you do?
choices: Enter the intersection anyway
Wait until you can clear the intersection without blocking it
Honk and push through
Back up to wait
questions.explanation: You should not enter an intersection on a green light unless you can clear it, to avoid blocking cross traffic.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the maximum speed limit on an interstate highway in Alabama?
choices: 55 mph
65 mph
70 mph
75 mph
questions.explanation: Alabama's default maximum speed on interstate highways is 70 mph unless signs say otherwise.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the maximum speed limit on a rural Alabama highway that is not an interstate and does not have four or more lanes?
choices: 45 mph
55 mph
65 mph
70 mph
questions.explanation: On two-lane rural highways in Alabama, the default maximum speed limit is 55 mph.

questions.prompt: What is the recommended minimum following distance behind the vehicle ahead under good conditions?
choices: One second
At least three to four seconds
Half a second
Ten seconds
questions.explanation: A following distance of at least three to four seconds gives you time to react and stop safely.

questions.prompt: How does higher speed affect the distance needed to stop your vehicle?
choices: It has no effect on stopping distance
It increases the distance needed to stop
It decreases the distance needed to stop
It only affects braking on hills
questions.explanation: The faster you go, the more distance your vehicle needs to stop, because both reaction and braking distance increase.

questions.prompt: What does the basic speed law require?
choices: Always driving the posted limit
Driving at a speed that is reasonable and safe for current conditions
Driving as fast as traffic allows
Driving 5 mph below the limit at all times
questions.explanation: The basic speed law requires you to adjust your speed to be safe for the conditions, even below the posted limit.

questions.prompt: In residential and urban areas without posted signs, the speed limit is typically what?
choices: 10 to 15 mph
25 to 30 mph
45 mph
55 mph
questions.explanation: Residential and urban areas in Alabama usually carry a default limit of about 25 to 30 mph.

questions.prompt: When should you increase your following distance?
choices: Only in heavy traffic
In rain, fog, or at higher speeds
Never, three seconds is always enough
Only when towing a trailer
questions.explanation: Poor weather and higher speeds require a larger gap because stopping takes longer.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic is dangerous because it does what?
choices: Saves fuel without any risk
Blocks traffic and surprises other drivers
Is always legal and safe
Improves your reaction time
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly impedes the flow of traffic and can cause crashes by surprising faster drivers.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the maximum speed limit on an Alabama highway with four or more lanes that is not an interstate?
choices: 55 mph
65 mph
70 mph
45 mph
questions.explanation: Alabama sets the default maximum at 65 mph on non-interstate highways having four or more lanes.

questions.prompt: What is the safest way to handle a posted speed limit during heavy rain?
choices: Always drive exactly the posted limit
Drive below the posted limit at a safe speed for conditions
Drive faster to get out of the rain
Stop in the travel lane
questions.explanation: The posted limit is the maximum for ideal conditions; in rain you must slow down for safety.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, the left lane is primarily intended for what?
choices: Slow-moving vehicles
Passing and faster-moving traffic
Parking
Turning right
questions.explanation: On multilane roads you should keep right except to pass; the left lane is for passing and faster traffic.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow centerline on your side of the road means what?
choices: Passing is never allowed
You may pass when it is safe
The road is one-way
You must change lanes
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line on your side allows passing when the way ahead is clear.

questions.prompt: When is it never safe or legal to pass another vehicle?
choices: On a straight, flat road with clear visibility
On a hill or curve where you cannot see oncoming traffic
When a broken yellow line is on your side
On a multilane highway
questions.explanation: You must never pass where your view is blocked, such as on hills, curves, or near intersections.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should do what to check your blind spot?
choices: Rely only on your mirrors
Look over your shoulder
Close your eyes briefly
Honk your horn
questions.explanation: Mirrors do not show your blind spot, so glance over your shoulder before moving over.

questions.prompt: When may you exceed the posted speed limit to complete a pass?
choices: Whenever you are passing
Only on the interstate
You may never exceed the posted limit
Only at night
questions.explanation: The speed limit applies even while passing; you may not break it to overtake another vehicle.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, when is it safe to return to your lane?
choices: Immediately after pulling alongside
When you can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
Only after the next intersection
As soon as you signal
questions.explanation: Returning to your lane only when you can see the passed vehicle in your mirror ensures you have enough room.

questions.prompt: On a road with two or more lanes in your direction, slower drivers should generally do what?
choices: Stay in the left lane
Stay in the right lane
Change lanes frequently
Drive on the shoulder
questions.explanation: Slower traffic should keep to the right and use the left lane only to pass.

questions.prompt: You should not pass within how many feet of a railroad crossing where your view is limited?
choices: 10 feet
50 feet
100 feet
500 feet
questions.explanation: Passing is prohibited within 100 feet of a railroad crossing where you cannot clearly see oncoming traffic.

questions.prompt: When another driver is passing you, what is the safest action?
choices: Speed up to prevent the pass
Maintain your speed or ease off so the pass finishes quickly
Move into the left lane
Brake hard
questions.explanation: Holding your speed or slowing slightly lets the passing driver complete the maneuver safely.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with siren and flashing lights, what should you do?
choices: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull over to the right and stop until it passes
Stop immediately in your lane
Continue at the same speed
questions.explanation: You must pull to the right edge of the road and stop to let an emergency vehicle pass.

questions.prompt: Under Alabama's Move Over Act, what must you do when approaching a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights on a road with two or more lanes in your direction?
choices: Move out of the lane closest to it
Speed up to pass quickly
Stop in your lane
Turn off your headlights
questions.explanation: The Move Over Act requires you to vacate the lane nearest the stopped emergency vehicle when you can do so safely.

questions.prompt: Under Alabama's Move Over Act, if you cannot safely change lanes away from a stopped emergency vehicle, what must you do?
choices: Maintain the posted speed
Slow to at least 15 mph below the posted speed limit
Stop completely in the road
Honk your horn
questions.explanation: If you cannot move over, the law requires slowing to at least 15 mph under the posted limit.

questions.prompt: Why should you be especially careful when sharing the road with motorcycles?
choices: They are slower than all other vehicles
They are smaller and harder to see
They always have the right-of-way
They cannot stop quickly
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are small and easily hidden in blind spots, so check carefully before turning or changing lanes.

questions.prompt: What must you do when a school bus displays flashing red lights and an extended stop arm?
choices: Slow down but keep moving
Stop and remain stopped until the lights stop flashing
Pass on the left quickly
Honk and proceed
questions.explanation: You must stop for a school bus loading or unloading children and wait until the red lights are off.

questions.prompt: Large trucks and buses require what compared to passenger cars?
choices: Much shorter stopping distances
Much longer distances to stop
No blind spots
Less following distance
questions.explanation: Heavy vehicles need far more distance to stop and have large blind spots, so give them extra room.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a bicyclist, you should do what?
choices: Pass closely to save time
Give them room and treat them as a road user with rights
Honk to make them move onto the sidewalk
Ignore them
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have the right to use the road, so pass with adequate space and patience.

questions.prompt: What is the safest practice when following an emergency vehicle that is responding to a call?
choices: Follow closely to clear traffic
Stay well back and never follow closely
Pass it on the right
Match its speed in the next lane
questions.explanation: Following an emergency vehicle too closely is dangerous and illegal; keep a safe distance behind.

questions.prompt: When turning at an intersection, you should check for what besides cars?
choices: Only traffic signals
Pedestrians and bicyclists who may be crossing
Nothing else
Parked cars only
questions.explanation: Turning drivers must watch for and yield to pedestrians and bicyclists who may be crossing their path.

questions.prompt: What is the legal BAC limit for a driver age 21 or older in Alabama?
choices: 0.05 percent
0.08 percent
0.10 percent
0.02 percent
questions.explanation: In Alabama it is illegal for a driver 21 or older to operate a vehicle with a BAC of 0.08 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: Under Alabama's zero-tolerance law, what BAC makes it illegal for a driver under 21 to drive?
choices: 0.08 percent or higher
0.02 percent or higher
0.05 percent or higher
0.10 percent or higher
questions.explanation: Drivers under 21 face a strict 0.02 percent limit under Alabama's zero-tolerance policy.

questions.prompt: Under Alabama's hands-free law, what may a driver do with a phone while driving?
choices: Hold it to send a text
Use hands-free features or a single touch or swipe
Watch videos while holding it
Browse the web while holding it
questions.explanation: Alabama's hands-free law bans holding a phone but allows hands-free use or a single touch or swipe.

questions.prompt: What happens if you refuse a lawful chemical test for alcohol after being arrested for impaired driving in Alabama?
choices: Nothing happens
Your license is automatically suspended
You receive a warning only
You must pay a small fee
questions.explanation: Under Alabama's implied-consent law, refusing a chemical test leads to an automatic license suspension.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect your driving ability?
choices: It improves your reaction time
It slows reactions and impairs judgment and vision
It has no effect in small amounts
It only affects you the next day
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reflexes and impairs judgment and vision, making driving far more dangerous.

questions.prompt: Which is the safest way to handle your phone while driving?
choices: Hold it low so police cannot see
Put it away until you have parked
Text only at red lights
Watch video with one hand
questions.explanation: The safest choice is to put the phone away entirely until you are parked.

questions.prompt: Besides alcohol, what else can impair your ability to drive safely?
choices: Only illegal drugs
Prescription and over-the-counter medications
Drinking water
Listening to the radio at low volume
questions.explanation: Many legal medications can cause drowsiness or slowed reactions and impair driving.

questions.prompt: What is the only thing that can sober up a person who has been drinking?
choices: Coffee
A cold shower
Time
Exercise
questions.explanation: Only time allows the body to remove alcohol; coffee and other remedies do not work.

questions.prompt: Which is an example of a distraction that increases crash risk?
choices: Keeping both hands on the wheel
Texting or reading a message while driving
Scanning the road ahead
Checking your mirrors
questions.explanation: Texting takes your eyes, hands, and attention off the road and greatly raises crash risk.

questions.prompt: In fog or heavy rain, which headlights should you use?
choices: High beams
Low beams
No headlights
Parking lights only
questions.explanation: Low beams work best in fog and heavy rain because high beams reflect off the moisture and reduce visibility.

questions.prompt: In Alabama, when are you required to turn on your headlights?
choices: Only on the interstate
From sunset to sunrise and whenever you cannot see clearly
Only when it is raining
Only in winter
questions.explanation: Headlights are required from sunset to sunrise and any time visibility is poor.

questions.prompt: When you turn on your windshield wipers because of weather, it is good practice to also do what?
choices: Turn on your low-beam headlights
Turn on your high beams
Turn off your headlights
Use your hazard lights
questions.explanation: Using low-beam headlights with your wipers helps you see and helps others see you in bad weather.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, what should you do?
choices: Slam on the brakes
Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
Steer the opposite way of the skid
Accelerate hard
questions.explanation: To recover from a skid, ease off the accelerator and steer gently toward where you want the front of the car to go.

questions.prompt: What should you do if your vehicle begins to hydroplane on standing water?
choices: Brake hard
Ease off the accelerator and keep the wheel straight
Turn sharply
Speed up to clear the water
questions.explanation: When hydroplaning, ease off the gas and hold the wheel straight until your tires regain traction.

questions.prompt: Why is the road especially slippery during the first rain after a dry spell?
choices: The pavement gets colder
Oil rises to the surface and mixes with water
Tires lose air pressure
Rain freezes immediately
questions.explanation: The first rain lifts oil and grime to the surface, making roads especially slick until it washes away.

questions.prompt: When driving in fog, in addition to using low beams you should do what?
choices: Speed up to get through it
Slow down and increase your following distance
Use high beams
Follow closely behind another car
questions.explanation: Reduced visibility in fog calls for slower speeds and a larger gap between vehicles.

questions.prompt: When should you reduce your speed for a wet curve?
choices: In the middle of the curve
Before you enter the curve
After you exit the curve
Only if other cars are present
questions.explanation: Slow down before entering a curve rather than braking in the middle, where you could lose traction.

questions.prompt: How far from a fire hydrant must you park in Alabama?
choices: At least 5 feet
At least 15 feet
At least 30 feet
At least 50 feet
questions.explanation: You may not park within 15 feet of a fire hydrant so firefighters can reach it.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, which way should you turn your front wheels?
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb when facing downhill stops the car from rolling into traffic if the brakes fail.

questions.prompt: Who must wear a properly fastened safety belt in a moving vehicle in Alabama?
choices: No one is required to
Each front-seat occupant
Only the driver under 18
Only passengers in the back seat
questions.explanation: Alabama law requires every front-seat occupant to wear a properly fastened safety belt while the vehicle is in motion.

questions.prompt: Where is it illegal to park?
choices: In a marked parking space
On a crosswalk or sidewalk
In a driveway you own
In a parking garage
questions.explanation: Parking on crosswalks, sidewalks, or within intersections is prohibited because it blocks others and creates hazards.

questions.prompt: What must a child be secured in while riding in a vehicle in Alabama?
choices: Any seat with a belt
An appropriate child safety seat or booster based on age and size
The driver's lap
No restraint is needed for short trips
questions.explanation: Children must ride in an age- and size-appropriate child safety seat or booster under Alabama law.

questions.prompt: When may you park in a marked accessible (disabled) parking space?
choices: When you are in a hurry
Only with a valid disability permit
For up to five minutes
When no other spaces are open
questions.explanation: Accessible parking spaces may only be used by vehicles displaying a valid disability permit or plate.

questions.prompt: After parking, what should you always do before leaving the vehicle?
choices: Leave it in neutral
Set the parking brake and leave it in park or in gear
Leave the engine running
Roll down all the windows
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake and leaving the car in park or in gear keeps it from rolling away.

questions.prompt: What is the single most effective thing you can do to protect yourself in a crash?
choices: Drive a large vehicle
Wear your safety belt
Use cruise control
Keep the radio off
questions.explanation: Buckling up is the most effective single action to reduce injury in a crash.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a Stage I learner's permit in Alabama?
questions.explanation: You may apply for an Alabama Stage I learner's permit at age 15.

questions.prompt: While holding an Alabama learner's permit, who must be in the vehicle with you?
choices: Any licensed driver
A licensed driver at least 21 years old seated in the front seat
A parent only
No one is required
questions.explanation: A learner's permit holder must be accompanied by a licensed driver age 21 or older in the front seat.

questions.prompt: Under Alabama's GDL law, a 16-year-old with a Stage II restricted license generally may not drive during which hours unless accompanied or for an exempt reason?
choices: Between 8 p.m. and 5 a.m.
Between midnight and 6 a.m.
Between 10 p.m. and 4 a.m.
There is no night restriction
questions.explanation: A Stage II driver may not drive between midnight and 6 a.m. unless accompanied by a parent or guardian or for an exempt purpose.

questions.prompt: How long must an under-18 driver hold a Stage I learner's permit before moving to a Stage II restricted license?
choices: At least 30 days
At least 6 months
At least 1 year
At least 2 years
questions.explanation: Drivers under 18 must hold the learner's permit for at least six months before getting a Stage II license.

questions.prompt: Under the Stage II restricted license, how many non-family passengers may you carry besides your supervising adult?
choices: No more than one
No more than three
Up to five
Any number
questions.explanation: A Stage II driver may carry no more than one non-family passenger besides a supervising licensed adult.

questions.prompt: What must you always do with your license or permit while driving?
choices: Leave it at home for safekeeping
Carry it and show it to law enforcement on request
Keep it only in your car's glovebox at all times
Memorize the number instead
questions.explanation: You must carry your license or permit while driving and present it to law enforcement when asked.

questions.prompt: At what age may an Alabama driver earn a Stage III unrestricted license after meeting the requirements?
questions.explanation: At age 17, after holding a Stage II license for six months with a clean record, a driver may earn the unrestricted Stage III license.

questions.prompt: If you move, what must you do about your address with ALEA?
choices: Nothing is required
Keep your address current
Wait until renewal to update it
Only update it if you move out of state
questions.explanation: You are responsible for keeping your address current with ALEA so official notices reach you.

questions.prompt: What does Alabama use to track and respond to repeated traffic violations?
choices: A point system
A reward system
A lottery
A mileage log
questions.explanation: Alabama adds points to your record for violations, and too many points can suspend your license.

questions.prompt: What can happen if you accumulate too many points on your driving record within a set period?
choices: Nothing changes
Your driving privileges can be suspended
You earn a discount
Your license is upgraded
questions.explanation: Accumulating too many points within the set period can lead to suspension of your driving privileges.

questions.prompt: A first violation of Alabama's hands-free law results in what?
choices: A warning only
A fine and one point on your record
Immediate license revocation
A jail sentence
questions.explanation: A first hands-free law violation carries a fine and one point added to your driving record.

questions.prompt: When stopped by law enforcement, what is the proper action?
choices: Get out and walk to the officer
Pull over safely to the right, stay in the car, and keep hands visible
Drive to the nearest parking lot far away
Refuse to roll down your window
questions.explanation: Pull over to the right, remain in your vehicle, and keep your hands visible when stopped by police.

questions.prompt: What documents must you show law enforcement when requested during a traffic stop?
choices: Only your license
License, registration, and proof of insurance
Your vehicle title only
A utility bill
questions.explanation: You must provide your driver license, vehicle registration, and proof of insurance on request.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a serious offense that can lead to license suspension or revocation and jail time?
choices: Driving under the influence
Parking too far from the curb
Driving 2 mph under the limit
Using your turn signal
questions.explanation: DUI is a serious offense bringing large fines, license suspension or revocation, and possible jail time.

questions.prompt: Driving without required insurance in Alabama can result in what?
choices: No consequences
Fines and further penalties
A free insurance policy
An automatic license upgrade
questions.explanation: Driving without the required insurance is a violation that can bring fines and additional penalties.

questions.prompt: Failing to stop for a school bus that is loading or unloading children can result in what?
choices: A reward
Fines and points on your record
Nothing, it is only a courtesy
A warning only every time
questions.explanation: Illegally passing a stopped school bus carries fines and points because it endangers children.

questions.prompt: By driving on Alabama roads, you give implied consent to what if lawfully arrested for impaired driving?
choices: Vehicle confiscation
Chemical testing for alcohol or drugs
A new driving test
Paying all court costs in advance
questions.explanation: Alabama's implied-consent law means a lawful arrest for impaired driving obligates you to submit to chemical testing.

questions.prompt: If you are driving at night and an oncoming vehicle's high beams blind you, what should you do?
choices: Look directly at the oncoming lights
Look toward the right edge of your lane
Turn on your own high beams
Close your eyes briefly
questions.explanation: Looking toward the right edge of your lane helps you stay on course without being blinded by oncoming lights.

questions.prompt: Where should you avoid lingering when driving near a large truck?
choices: In front of it with plenty of space
In its blind spots alongside and directly behind it
Two lanes away
On a different road
questions.explanation: Large trucks have big blind spots on the sides and directly behind, so do not linger where the driver cannot see you.

questions.prompt: What is the main reason a posted speed limit represents a maximum rather than a target?
choices: It is only a suggestion
It assumes ideal conditions, which often are not present
It applies only to trucks
It changes every hour
questions.explanation: Posted limits assume ideal conditions, so you must drive slower when weather, traffic, or visibility is poor.

questions.prompt: From a one-way street onto another one-way street, when may you turn left on a red light in Alabama?
choices: Never
After stopping and yielding, if no sign prohibits it
Without stopping
Only at night
questions.explanation: Alabama allows a left turn on red from a one-way onto a one-way street after a complete stop and yielding, unless prohibited.

questions.prompt: What should you do before you begin to pass another vehicle?
choices: Signal, check mirrors, and check your blind spot
Speed up without signaling
Flash your headlights only
Close the gap as much as possible
questions.explanation: Always signal, check your mirrors, and clear your blind spot before pulling out to pass.

questions.prompt: What is the best way to prevent impaired driving if you plan to drink?
choices: Drink only beer
Arrange a sober driver or another ride in advance
Drive slowly afterward
Wait 15 minutes before driving
questions.explanation: Planning a sober ride in advance is the reliable way to avoid driving while impaired.

questions.prompt: What does a white regulatory sign, such as a speed limit sign, tell you?
choices: A warning about the road ahead
The law you must obey
The name of a city
A point of interest
questions.explanation: White regulatory signs state laws you must follow, such as posted speed limits.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, which way should you turn your front wheels?
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Facing uphill with a curb, turn the wheels away from the curb so the tires catch the curb if the car rolls back.

questions.prompt: Which agency issues driver licenses and permits in Alabama?
choices: The Alabama Law Enforcement Agency (ALEA)
The county tax office
The U.S. Department of Transportation
The Social Security Administration
questions.explanation: The Alabama Law Enforcement Agency's Driver License Division issues driver licenses and permits in Alabama.
